message:
fix(rpc/xdr): xdr_pmaplist iterative rewrite, no recursion DoS (bd-lc5ss)

Old `xdr_pmaplist` delegated to `xdr_pointer` which recursed into
`xdr_pmap_list_entry`, which recursed back into `xdr_pointer` for the
next list entry. A wire stream consisting of N "more=1" flags
followed by minimal Pmap data consumed N stack frames — at default 8
MiB stack, ~50_000 entries (~1 MiB of wire) overflows. SIGSEGV →
pre-auth DoS for any service that decodes pmaplist over the wire
(rpcbind, NFS portmapper consumers, NIS+).

Glibc's `xdr_pmaplist` (sunrpc/pmap_prot2.c) is iterative.
Reimplement to match: walk pml_next pointers in a single loop,
calling `xdr_reference` per entry to alloc/encode/free the 40-byte
PmapList struct and `xdr_pmap` for the 32-byte map payload. Two
subtleties:

- The XDR_FREE pass walks down a list whose entries are freed inside
  xdr_reference, so we stash the next-pointer LOCATION (not value)
  in `saved_next` BEFORE the call to avoid a UAF when stepping.
- xdr_reference allocates `sizeof(PmapList)` (40) but xdr_pmap only
  initializes the first 32 (pm_prog..pm_port). Explicitly zero
  pml_next after each xdr_reference so the encode-side `*cursor`
  peek and the tail-of-list `*cursor = null` write always target
  initialized memory.

Add `xdr_pmaplist_deep_stream_does_not_overflow_stack` regression
test: 50,000-entry stream (~1 MiB) decoded then freed via
`xdr_pmaplist` with XDR_FREE. Recursive impl SIGSEGVs on the
decoder; iterative impl runs in ~10 ms.

Verified: 86/86 xdr_abi_test pass on default thread count + 3 stress
iterations.
